metronomeSettings.title: Metronome
metronomeSettings.forceLoopBeat1Desc: When activating a loop, it always restarts from beat 1, regardless of when it was triggered.
metronomeSettings.accentDesc: Disable to remove the emphasis (high-pitched tone) on the first beat of each measure.
metronomeSettings.tip: The "Loop always on beat 1" option is ideal for worship, ensuring the loop is always synchronized with the measure even when triggered off-beat.

help.title: Help Center
help.subtitle: How can we help?
help.question: Your question or issue
help.heroSubtitle: Find detailed tutorials, guides with real screenshots and answers to get the most out of Glory Pads.
help.quickTipText: Inside the app, tap the {{icon}} icon in the header to start an interactive tutorial that highlights each feature directly on screen, with a step-by-step guide.
help.faq0a: Yes! After loading the sounds for the first time, they are stored locally on your device via IndexedDB. You can play even without internet.
help.faq1q: How to import sounds from Glory Store?
help.faq1a: Hold a pad, select "Import from Glory Store" and choose the desired sound from purchased packs. The sound will be downloaded and saved locally on the device.
help.faq2q: Can I use a MIDI controller?
help.faq2a: Yes! Connect a MIDI controller via USB or Bluetooth. The app detects it automatically. Access MIDI settings in the menu to map notes and CCs.
help.faq3a: In the setlist, tap the share icon for the event. A public link will be generated so other musicians can see the songs, BPMs and keys.
help.faq4q: My metronome has no sound, what to do?
help.faq4a: Check if the device's silent mode is off and if the metronome volume in the mixer (page 1 fader) is above zero.
help.faq5q: Do audio effects drain more battery?
help.faq5a: Minimally. The effects (EQ, Reverb, Delay) use the browser's native Web Audio API, optimized for real-time performance.
help.faq6q: How do I save a song's settings?
help.faq6a: When adding a song to the setlist, all settings (BPM, volumes, pans, effects, sounds, MIDI) are saved automatically. When reloading the song, everything is restored.
help.faq7a: The Free plan offers basic features. Pro unlocks more pads and imports. Master gives access to audio effects (EQ, Reverb, Delay) and advanced features.
help.catPadsDesc: The main app screen. Pads are interactive buttons that play drum, percussion and loop sounds on touch.
help.catSetlistDesc: The setlist system lets you organize songs by event (service, rehearsal, show), automatically saving all settings for each song.
help.catAmbientDesc: Sustained note pads that create continuous sound atmospheres. Ideal for worship moments, prayer and transitions.
help.catMixerDesc: The fader mixer provides granular volume control for each pad, metronome, continuous pads and master output.
help.catMetronomeDesc: Built-in metronome with BPM control, time signature, loop sync and visual beat indicator.
help.catMidiDesc: Connect MIDI controllers (USB or Bluetooth) and map pads, volumes, BPM and song navigation.
help.catEffectsDesc: Apply 3-band EQ, Reverb and BPM-synced Delay to each pad individually. Master plan feature.
help.catModesDesc: Edit Mode to quickly configure pads and Focus Mode to maximize the touch area.
help.catStoreDesc: Professional sound pack store. Purchase, download and import directly to your pads.
help.artPlayingPurpose: The pads work as a portable electronic drum. Each pad plays a different sound — kicks, snares, hi-hats, claps, crashes and more. It's the main way to play on Glory Pads.
help.artPlayingS0D: Simply tap any pad to hear the corresponding sound. The pad color indicates the sound category (red = kick, orange = snare, yellow = hi-hat, etc).
help.artPlayingS1D: Long press on any pad to open the options menu: adjust individual volume, pan (left/right), rename the pad, change its color, apply audio effects and import sounds from Glory Store.
help.artPlayingS2D: In the menu (≡), use the – and + buttons to decrease or increase pad size. Useful for adapting to the number of pads you need to see.
help.artPlayingS3D: Pads are organized in a 3×3 grid per page. Navigate between pages (1, 2, 3) to access all available pads.
help.artLoopsPurpose: Loops are pre-recorded rhythmic patterns that play on continuous repeat, synced to the metronome BPM. They provide a consistent rhythmic base without manual playing.
help.artLoopsS0D: Loop pads (identified by names like WSP, WFL) work as toggles: tap once to start the loop and tap again to stop. While active, the pad shows an animated border.
help.artLoopsS1D: Loops automatically adjust to the BPM set in the metronome. If you change BPM from 120 to 90, the loop follows the change in real time.
help.artLoopsS2D: Loops always start aligned to the kick (beat 1) of the measure to maintain perfect musical sync, regardless of when you tap the pad.
help.artEventsPurpose: Events organize your songs for each occasion. Create events like "Sunday Service", "Wednesday Rehearsal" or "Conference" and add setlist songs to each one. The app remembers the last selected event.
help.artEventsS0D: Tap "Setlist" in the header and then "+ New Event". Set a name (e.g., Sunday Service) and the event date.
help.artEventsS1D: Inside the event, tap "+ Song" to add. Each song automatically saves: BPM, key, individual pad volumes, pans, audio effects, custom sounds and MIDI mappings.
help.artEventsS2D: Tap the song name inside the event to load it. All pads, volumes, effects and settings will be restored instantly.
help.artEventsS3D: Use the ◀ ▶ buttons in the header when an event is active to navigate between songs without opening the setlist. Also works via MIDI CC.
help.artSharePurpose: Share the event setlist with other band members via public link. They can see the song list, BPMs and keys — useful for rehearsals and preparation.
help.artShareS0D: In the event, tap the share icon. A link will be generated that can be sent via WhatsApp, email or any messenger.
help.artShareS1D: Musicians who access it will see: event name, date, song list with BPM and key for each one. No app account required.
help.artContinuousPurpose: Continuous Pads generate sustained musical notes (like keyboard pads) that play continuously. They create worship atmospheres, fill sonic spaces and make smooth transitions between songs.
help.artContinuousS0D: In the "Pads" footer tab, expand the "Continuous Pads" section. Tap any note (C, D, E, F...) to activate it. Tap again to deactivate. Multiple notes can sound simultaneously to create chords.
help.artContinuousS1D: Use the vertical slider on the left to adjust the continuous pads volume independently from drum pads.
help.artContinuousS2D: The orange knob controls sound direction: turn to L (left) or R (right). Useful for separating ambient pads from drum sound in stage monitors.
help.artMixerPurpose: The mixer works like a digital mixing console. It allows adjusting individual volume for each pad, metronome and continuous pads, plus controlling master (overall) volume. Essential for balancing sounds live.
help.artMixerS0D: The mixer is divided into 3 pages (1, 2, 3) to accommodate all faders. Page 1 contains metronome, main PAD and first pads. Navigate between pages by tapping the numbers.
help.artMixerS1D: Drag each fader up/down to adjust volume. The value appears below each fader (0-100). The "Master" fader controls the overall volume of all audio output.
help.artMixerS2D: The orange knob at the bottom directs all audio output to left (L) or right (R). Useful for sending app sound to only one side of monitors/headphones.
help.artMetronomePurpose: The metronome serves as a rhythmic reference to keep time during performance. It syncs loops, provides audible click and shows visual beat indicators. BPM and time signature are saved per song in the setlist.
help.artMetronomeS0D: Use the horizontal slider for quick adjustment, – / + buttons for fine-tuning, or tap directly on the BPM number to type an exact value.
help.artMetronomeS1D: Choose between 4/4, 3/4 or 6/8 by tapping the corresponding buttons. The time signature affects the metronome count and loop synchronization.
help.artMetronomeS2D: Tap the Play button (green) to start the metronome. When running, the button turns red (Stop) and visual indicators flash on each beat.
help.artMetronomeS3D: The "Sync" button (purple) syncs loop pads with the metronome rhythmic grid. When active, loops respect the exact beat timing.
help.artMetronomeS4D: The "Key" field lets you set the song's tonality (C, D, Em, etc). This info is saved automatically in the setlist and shown when sharing.
help.artMetronomeS5D: The "Metronome Pan" knob directs the click to L or R. Very useful for hearing the click separate from the band — send to only one side of headphones/monitors.
help.artMidiPurpose: MIDI integration allows using physical controllers (pads, pedalboards, keyboards) to play pads, adjust volumes and navigate between songs. Ideal for live performances where you need tactile control.
help.artMidiS0D: Connect a MIDI controller via USB or Bluetooth to your device. The app detects it automatically via Web MIDI API. A MIDI indicator appears when connected.
help.artMidiS1D: In MIDI settings, associate each controller note to a specific pad. Play the note on the controller and then select the target pad.
help.artMidiS2D: Continuous controls (CCs) can be mapped to: master volume, BPM, previous/next song navigation, and individual pad volume.
help.artMidiS3D: All MIDI mappings (notes, CCs and channels) are saved individually per song in the setlist. When switching songs, mappings are restored automatically.
help.artEffectsPurpose: Audio effects allow shaping each pad's sound individually. Use EQ to adjust frequencies (bass, mids, highs), Reverb for spatial depth and Delay for rhythmic repeats. Essential for polishing live sound.
help.artEffectsS0D: Long press on any pad and select "Effects" from the options menu. The effects panel opens with three sections: EQ, Reverb and Delay.
help.artEffectsS1D: Adjust Low (bass), Mid (mids) and High (highs) with individual sliders. Positive values boost, negative values cut. Useful for removing unwanted frequencies or enhancing the sound character.
help.artEffectsS2D: Adds a reverb tail to the sound, simulating environments like rooms, churches or halls. Adjust Mix (effect amount) and Decay (reverb duration).
help.artEffectsS3D: Enable "Sync BPM" so the delay time syncs with the metronome. Choose musical subdivisions (1/4, 1/8, 1/16) so repeats fall exactly on the beat.
help.artEditModePurpose: Edit Mode turns a simple tap into a shortcut to each pad's settings. Instead of long pressing, just tap any pad to open its options. Ideal for quickly configuring multiple pads.
help.artEditModeS0D: Open the menu (≡) and enable "Edit Mode". Pads will show a different visual indicator showing they're in configuration mode.
help.artEditModeS1D: With the mode active, tap any pad to open its options (volume, pan, color, effects, import). No need to long press.
help.artEditModeS2D: Go back to menu (≡) and disable "Edit Mode" to return to normal touch behavior (sound playback).
help.artFocusModePurpose: Focus Mode removes the header to maximize pad area. Perfect for live performances where you need the most space possible to play.
help.artFocusModeS0D: In the menu (≡), enable "Focus Mode". The header (with song name, navigation and menu) will be hidden, giving more vertical space for pads.
help.artFocusModeS1D: Even in focus mode, the metronome and Continuous Pads remain accessible via footer. You don't lose functionality.
help.artFocusModeS2D: Tap the header area (now reduced) or swipe down to restore the full view.
help.artBrowsingPurpose: Glory Store is where you find professional sound packs to expand your library. Packs include kicks, snares, hi-hats, loops, continuous pads, effects and more — all designed for worship.
help.artBrowsingS0D: Tap "Store" in the app footer or access via menu (≡) > "Glory Store". The store opens with catalogs organized by category.
help.artBrowsingS1D: Use the "Categories" button to filter by sound type: Drums, Loops (4/4, 3/4, 6/8), Continuous Pads, Effects and others.
help.artBrowsingS2D: Each pack has audio previews. Tap the play button on each sound to listen before buying.
help.artBrowsingS3D: Tap "Buy" on the pack card. Payment is processed securely. You must be logged in.
help.artImportingPurpose: After purchasing a pack, sounds are available to import to any pad. The sound is downloaded and saved locally, working offline.
help.artImportingS0D: Hold any pad > "Import from Glory Store". Filter by purchased pack and select the desired sound.
help.artImportingS1D: The sound is downloaded and stored locally on the device. After the first download, it works offline.
help.artImportingS2D: Your purchased packs appear in the "My Library" section in the store. Removed packs can be restored at any time.

notifications.activeDescription: You'll receive alerts even when the app is closed.
notifications.inactiveDescription: Enable to receive updates from Glory Pads.
notifications.blocked: Permission blocked by the system. To enable, go to your browser settings and allow notifications for this site.
notifications.notSupported: Push notifications are not supported in this browser. To receive notifications, install the app as PWA on your phone.
notifications.howItWorksDescription: When enabled, you'll receive important updates from the Glory Pads team directly on your phone or computer, even with the app closed. No spam.
notifications.testBody: Notifications working! You'll receive alerts even with the app closed.

tapTempo.title: Tap Tempo
tapTempo.autoApplyDescription: After you stop tapping, the detected BPM is automatically applied and returns to the Mix.
tapTempo.timeoutDescription: Time without taps to automatically apply BPM.
tapTempo.timeoutDetailDesc: Seconds of inactivity before applying the BPM. A red bar appears in the last seconds.
tapTempo.redirectAfterApplyDesc: Choose which screen to go to automatically after the BPM is applied.
tapTempo.howItWorksDesc: Tap the Tap Tempo button to the beat of the music. The app detects the BPM automatically. If auto-apply is on, after {{timeout}} seconds without tapping, the BPM is applied and you're redirected to {{target}}.

adminTranslations.title: Manage Translations
adminTranslations.description: Edit app translations per language. Changes are saved as overrides in the database.
adminTranslations.autoTranslateDesc: Use AI to translate all missing keys for the selected language.
adminTranslations.noMissing: All keys are already translated for this language!

midi.title: MIDI
midi.notSupported: MIDI is not supported in this browser. Use Chrome, Edge, or Android to connect MIDI controllers.
midi.connectController: Connect a MIDI controller via USB or Bluetooth
midi.noteChannelDesc: Filters MIDI notes for the pads.
midi.ccChannelDesc: Filters CC messages for volumes, BPM, etc.
midi.padMappingDesc: Associate MIDI notes to pads. Use "Learn" to map from your controller.
midi.ccMappingDesc: Associate CC controls (faders, knobs, buttons) to functions. Use "Learn" to map from your controller.
midi.howItWorksDesc: Connect a MIDI controller via USB or Bluetooth. The app detects it automatically and lets you play pads with velocity sensitivity. Use "Learn" mode to customize the mapping of each key, fader, or knob. Move the desired control on your controller to associate it with the function.

install.title: Glory Pads
install.description: Install the app on your phone for quick access, full screen and offline use.
install.iosStep1: 1. Tap the Share button (square with arrow icon) in Safari
install.iosStep2: 2. Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
install.manualStep2: 2. Tap "Install app" or "Add to Home Screen"
install.manualChrome: In Google Chrome, you can also tap "Share" and then "Add to Home Screen".

drumPad.importLimitHint: Remove a custom sound or upgrade.

bpmGuide.title: Import guide
bpmGuide.description: For best results, follow these tips when importing sounds:
bpmGuide.syncBpmDesc: Make sure the audio file is at the same BPM as the app's metronome to keep sync.
bpmGuide.formatsDesc: MP3, WAV and OGG. For best quality, use uncompressed WAV.
bpmGuide.shortSamplesDesc: Sounds of 1 to 4 measures work best. Very long files may cause delay on trigger.

banners.silentDesc: Turn off the iPhone side mute switch to hear sounds.

upgradeGate.availableFrom: This feature is available from the {{plan}} plan. Upgrade to unlock.

musicAI.noResults: No songs found. Try another search.
musicAI.lockedMaster: 🔒 Music AI available on Master plan

spotifySearch.title: Search on Spotify
spotifySearch.description: Search a song and AI analyzes the real audio to suggest effects (EQ, reverb, delay) on pads
spotifySearch.searchError: Error searching songs. Check Spotify credentials.

loopImport.title: Import Loop
loopImport.description: You are importing "{{name}}" into a loop pad. Enter the loop's BPM so the metronome is configured automatically.
loopImport.warning: For perfect sync, the imported loop must have exactly the same BPM as the metronome. Loops with a different BPM may sound out of time.
